sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund Dolos by TxPipe: Maintaining Cardano's Lightweight Data Node, Year 2, including maintenance, enhancement, community support, and AI-friendly documentation/integration resources over 12 months.
Recipient: 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v, submitted by Intersect on behalf of TxPipe as vendor

Deliverables: Part-time maintainer for Dolos over 12 months, Essential maintenance including dependency updates, Cardano protocol compatibility, performance improvements, bug fixing, and documentation, Community support including issue triage, review of external contributions, ecosystem-feedback-driven enhancements, and public developer communication, AI-friendly documentation and integration resources, Milestone-based disbursement controls through Intersect treasury management smart contracts
Deadline/expiry: 12 months; grant period begins after closure of existing contract

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 540,750 ADA total, composed of 525,000 ADA for Dolos maintenance and enhancement and a 15,750 ADA Intersect Budget Administration fee.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The motivation describes the underlying vendor funding as 420,000 ADA plus a 105,000 ADA contingency reserve for a part-time Dolos maintainer over 12 months at an annual rate of $105,000 USD.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Dolos is described as a lightweight Cardano data node written in Rust that provides efficient chain-data access without the overhead of traditional full nodes.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, medium materiality): Dolos is claimed to expose five query interfaces including Mini-Blockfrost, UTxO-RPC, Mini-Kupo, and an Ouroboros N2C unix socket compatible with Cardano-cli, Ogmios, and other tooling.

Claim (adoption, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): The document states that Dolos has 939 commits, 80 releases, 129 stars, 51 forks, and 27 contributors, and links to the Dolos GitHub repository.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposer claims Dolos aligns with Pillar 2 focus area A.3 on Developer Experience and open-source incentives for maintaining core Cardano SDKs, frameworks, and infrastructure.
missing: Independent evidence for: Dolos is described as a lightweight Cardano data node written in Rust that provides efficient chain-data access without the overhead of traditional full nodes.

Independent evidence for: The proposer claims Dolos aligns with Pillar 2 focus area A.3 on Developer Experience and open-source incentives for maintaining core Cardano SDKs, frameworks, and infrastructure.

Independent evidence for: The document states TxPipe has received funding through 30 Project Catalyst proposals, with 26 successfully delivered and 4 under development and on schedule, plus 2025 Intersect maintenance funding for Pallas, Dolos, and UTxO RPC being delivered on schedule.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 540,750 ADA total, composed of 525,000 ADA for Dolos maintenance and enhancement and a 15,750 ADA Intersect Budget Administration fee." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Dolos is described as a lightweight Cardano data node written in Rust that provides efficient chain-data access without the overhead of traditional full nodes." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
